perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that upon termination/expiry of this Agreement or at any time during its currency, at the request of the disclosing party, the receiving party shall promptly deliver to the disclosing party the Confidential Information and copies thereof in its possession or under its direct or indirect control, and shall destroy all memoranda, notes and other writings prepared by the receiving party or its Affiliates or directors, officers, employees or advisors based on the Confidential Information and promptly certify such destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that upon termination/expiry of this Agreement or at any time during its currency, at the request of the disclosing party, the receiving party shall make a reasonable effort to deliver to the disclosing party the Confidential Information and copies thereof in its possession or under its direct or indirect control, and shall make a reasonable effort to destroy all memoranda, notes and other writings prepared by the receiving party or its Affiliates or directors, officers, employees or advisors based on the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This weakens the obligation to return and destroy Confidential Information to a 'reasonable effort'. This is contradicting with Section 14 where it states that "In any event, however, the obligations of each Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information it has received under this agreement shall continue indefinitely", creating ambiguity about the receiving party's long-term obligations concerning this data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain valid for a period of three (3) years from the date of execution of this Agreement which term may be extended by mutual consent in writing of both the parties. This Agreement may be terminated by either party by giving thirty (30) days notice in writing to the other party without assigning any reason whatsoever. The obligations of each party hereunder will continue and be binding irrespective of whether the discussion between the parties materialise into a specific understanding/business relationship or not and for a further period of three (3) years after termination/expiry of the Agreement. In any event, however, the obligations of each Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information it has received under this agreement shall continue indefinitely as to Confidential Information constituting a trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation to return and destroy confidential information by changing 'shall promptly deliver' and 'shall destroy' to 'shall make a reasonable effort to deliver' and 'shall make a reasonable effort to destroy.' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that 'the obligations of each Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information it has received under this agreement shall continue indefinitely.' The introduction of 'reasonable effort' creates ambiguity and directly clashes with the indefinite obligation of confidentiality, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed exclusively by the laws of India and subject to Clause 11, jurisdiction shall be vested exclusively in the courts at Hyderabad.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be generally governed by the laws of India and jurisdiction may be vested in the courts at Hyderabad.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the governing law and jurisdiction less certain by replacing 'exclusively' with 'generally' and 'shall' with 'may'. This undermines the certainty of legal recourse, directly conflicting with the purpose of Section 11 which clearly defines the arbitration process in India. The lack of a clear exclusive jurisdiction causes a problem for the enforceability of the agreement and conflicting the whole point on the arbitration, since its validity is being put in question due to the modifications being made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any dispute arises between the parties hereto during the subsistence or thereafter, in connection with or arising out of this Agreement, the dispute shall be referred to arbitration under the Indian Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1995 by a panel of three arbitrators. Each party will appoint one arbitrator and the two arbitrators so appointed will appoint the third or the presiding arbitrator. Arbitration shall be held in Hyderabad, India. The proceedings of arbitration shall be in the English language. The arbitrator’s award shall be final and binding on the parties. 2
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the agreement would be 'exclusively' governed by the laws of India and that jurisdiction 'shall' be vested exclusively in the courts at Hyderabad. The changed text replaces 'exclusively' with 'generally' and 'shall' with 'may,' weakening the certainty of both the governing law and the jurisdiction. This introduces ambiguity. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' regarding arbitration, which clearly defines the arbitration process in Hyderabad, India, implying a definitive and predictable legal environment. By making the governing law and jurisdiction uncertain, the modification undermines the enforceability and validity of the arbitration clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined in Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.
